item.title: Xbox Plans Significant Layoffs as It Transforms Under New CEO
item.description: Asha Sharma’s first major cuts will arrive in July

item.title: Why 2026 Is Beginning to Look Like 1929
item.description: On Trumponomics, financial journalist Andrew Ross Sorkin argues the AI boom, crypto and regulatory rollback all echo the Roaring Twenties—and what came after.

item.title: Factory Robot Startup Mujin Raising Funds Ahead of IPO by 2030
item.description: Japanese robot software developer Mujin plans to go public by 2030, building up funds and momentum to capture fast-growing demand for factory-use AI.

item.title: Oracle’s Higher Data Center Spending Overshadows AI Growth
item.description: Oracle Corp. reported quarterly capital expenses that were higher than estimates, raising investor concerns about the profitability of the company’s AI infrastructure business.

item.title: Citi Says Investors Growing More Selective on Data Center Bonds
item.description: Bond investors are increasingly scrutinizing financing deals linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure buildouts, according to Citigroup Inc. analysts.

item.title: Latham’s Kelly Forecasts Private Equity-Fueled Second-Half Surge
item.description: Mega deals will likely continue driving an M&A resurgence that will accelerate in the second half of 2026, with private equity keeping the pace, predicts Alex Kelly of Latham & Watkins.

item.title: OpenAI Says China-Linked Accounts Aim to Fuel US Data Center Pushback
item.description: OpenAI said a group of ChatGPT accounts with ties to China have sought to stir up local opposition to data centers in the US in a potential bid to hinder the country’s competitiveness in artificial intelligence, echoing recent rhetoric from others in the tech industry.

item.title: SpaceX Tells Investors It Has Lined Up Blue-Chip Credit Ratings
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is telling investors it has lined up investment-grade ratings from three major bond graders, according to people with knowledge of the matter, which could help the company cut funding costs as it continues to raise financing after its initial public offering.

item.title: Chevron Among Drillers to Feed Key Argentina Shale NGL Venture
item.description: Chevron Corp. and two other major Argentine shale producers will sign contracts this week to supply a natural gas liquids project, a move that all but assures the $3 billion plan goes ahead, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Major US Banks Face Federal Probe Over Debanking Allegations
item.description: Federal prosecutors are looking into whether some of Wall Street’s biggest lenders illegally dropped customers for political reasons, according to a person familiar with the probe.

item.title: Musk Stock Fans Say ‘The More, The Better’ in SpaceX IPO Frenzy
item.description: Anna Watts, a 33-year-old public relations manager in New York, has stashed away $6,500 to buy SpaceX stock after it hits the market Friday. If she had her way, she’d buy even more. She tried to borrow $5,000 from her best friend and applied for a bank loan, too, but both turned her away.

item.title: US Refunds $22 Billion in Tariffs, Canceling Out Customs Revenue
item.description: The US Treasury refunded nearly $22 billion in tariff revenue collected from importers in May, the first swath of such repayments since the Supreme Court struck down a major component of President Donald Trump’s trade policy.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Says Government Should Be Able to Block New Models
item.description: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei said the government should have the power to block artificial intelligence developers from deploying new AI models if they present certain risks.

item.title: AI Data Firm DDN Eyeing a Fresh Funding Round by End of Year
item.description: DDN, a data hardware and software provider that counts Nvidia Corp. as a partner and Alphabet Inc.’s Google and Salesforce Inc. as customers, is looking to raise additional funding by the end of the year, Chief Executive Officer Alex Bouzari said.

item.title: SpaceX Leaves Some Banks Peeved at Junior Roles in IPO Lineup
item.description: Several junior banks marketing shares in SpaceX’s landmark listing are working for relatively small fees and being relegated to roles that don’t come with any credit, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CoreWeave’s Credit Rebound Drives Cheaper Data Center Funding
item.description: When Applied Digital Corp. first tapped the junk-bond market in November to fund a data center project tied to CoreWeave Inc., it had to stomach a hefty yield to get the deal done. Fast forward to this week, and borrowing costs for another portion of the same project tumbled.

item.title: UK’s Reeves to Lower Tax Burden for Wealthy US Expats
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said she will ease the tax burden faced by rich US investors wanting to relocate to the UK.

item.title: New AI Research Offers Warning as US Embraces Its Use in Warfare
item.description: Findings serve as warning as Trump administration embraces AI in warfare

item.title: Amazon’s Record Bond Deal Shakes Up Canada’s Credit Market
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s record loonie bond sale has the Canadian fixed income market working hard to accommodate the deluge of debt, pushing risk spreads higher and spurring other borrowers to delay note sales of their own.

item.title: Sitting Out the SpaceX IPO? Why That’s Hard to Do
item.description: Wall Street has been fixated on the stock market debut of Elon Musk’s SpaceX. The rocket-and-AI company’s initial public offering on June 12 is set to be the largest in history.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Is Said to Be More Than Four Times Oversubscribed
item.description: SpaceX’s initial public offering has attracted demand for more than four times the available shares, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of the Elon Musk-led rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence firm stopping taking orders.

item.title: Creative Artists, TPG Back $250 Million Fund for YouTube Talent
item.description: Creative Artists Agency, Hollywood’s biggest talent representative, is starting a new company to invest in YouTube stars and podcasters who want to be the next Oprah Winfrey or Reese Witherspoon.

item.title: Drugmaker Parabilis Soars 67% After $745 Million IPO, Placement
item.description: Parabilis Medicines Inc. shares jumped as much as 67% in its trading debut, after the company raised nearly $745 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above its marketed range and a private placement.

item.title: SpaceX Holder Says Orbital Data-Centers Offer Big Payoff, Risks
item.description: SpaceX’s $1.75 trillion initial public offering highlights the potential upside — and downside — from Elon Musk’s bold vision to place data centers in Earth orbit, according to one of the company’s private investors.

item.title: Ashley’s Frasers Offers to Buy Hugo Boss in $3.1 Billion Deal
item.description: Frasers Group Plc offered to buy Hugo Boss AG in a deal valuing the German fashion brand at about €2.7 billion ($3.1 billion), as billionaire Mike Ashley seeks to add another well-known label to his growing collection.

item.title: Oil Crunch Is Stoking Asia’s Demand for Coal, Shipping CEO Says
item.description: Coal is emerging as an increasingly attractive alternative to oil for energy-starved countries across Asia, according to Stamatis Tsantanis, chief executive officer of Seanergy Maritime Holdings Corp.

item.title: ASML to Cut Fewer Jobs Than Planned After Union Negotiations
item.description: Chip-equipment maker ASML Holding NV will cut fewer jobs than planned, with redundancies not expected to take effect until May 1, following negotiations with Dutch trade unions.

item.title: Trump Says US Won’t Renew USMCA, Signaling Yearly Reviews
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would not reauthorize the US’s trade deal with Canada and Mexico, setting the stage for months or years of negotiations over provisions governing automobiles and other key industries.

item.title: Russia Restores Access to Roblox Gaming Platform, Interfax Says
item.description: Russia restored access to Roblox Corp.’s gaming platform after concluding the company had complied with local legal requirements, according to Interfax.

item.title: Tether Backs German Robotics Startup Neura in $1.4 Billion Round
item.description: German startup Neura Robotics has raised about $1.4 billion in a funding round backed by stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA to develop and ship its artificial intelligence-powered humanoid robot.
